1 ble individuals and families. Such funds are to be made available to
2 establish a career pathways program to link education and occupa-
3 tional training to subsequent employment through a continuum of
4 educational programs and integrated support services to enable
5 eligible participants, including disconnected young adults, ages
6 sixteen to twenty-four, to advance over time both to higher levels
7 of education and to higher wage jobs in targeted occupational
8 sectors. With funds appropriated herein, the office of temporary and
9 disability assistance in consultation with the department of labor
10 shall establish the career pathways program and provide technical
11 support, as needed, to provide education, training, and job place-
12 ment for low-income individuals, age sixteen and older. Preference
13 shall be given to eighteen to twenty-four year olds who are unem-
14 ployed or underemployed, in areas of the state with demonstrated
15 labor market needs and unemployment rates that are greater than the
16 appropriate or comparative rate of employment for the region, and to
17 persons in receipt of family assistance and/or safety net assist-
18 ance. Of the amounts appropriated, to the extent practicable, at
19 least sixty percent shall be available for services to eighteen to
20 twenty-four year olds, with remaining funds available to recipients
21 of family assistance and/or safety net assistance, without age
22 restrictions, and sixteen to seventeen year old self-supporting
23 individuals who are heads of household. The office of temporary and
24 disability assistance in consultation with the department of labor
25 shall develop a request for proposals and shall receive, review, and
26 assess applications. In selecting proposals, the office of temporary
27 and disability assistance and the department of labor shall give
28 preference to programs that demonstrate community-based collab-

bill_language:
7 By chapter 53, section 1, of the laws of 2023:
8 For services and expenses of an emergency rental assistance program.
9 Households eligible for assistance under such program shall include
10 one or more individuals that has experienced financial hardship, is
11 at risk of homelessness or housing instability, and earns up to
12 eighty percent of area median income as determined by the United
13 States department of housing and urban development. Such assistance
14 shall support the payment of up to 12 months of rental arrears due
15 at the time of application and up to 3 months of prospective rent
16 pursuant to part BB of chapter 56 of the laws of 2021, as amended by
17 chapter 417 of the laws of 2021, federal law and other purposes set
18 forth in Public Law No. 116-260, Public Law 117-2, or any other
19 federal funds made available for this purpose.
20 Notwithstanding any other law to the contrary, these funds shall be
21 available for applications received on or before January 20,
22 2023, including for tenants or occupants of federal- or state-
23 funded subsidized public housing authorities or other federal- or
24 state-funded subsidized housing that limits the household's share
25 of the rent to a set percentage of income.
26 Notwithstanding any other law to the contrary, any funds beyond what
27 are needed to fund such applications received on or before January
28 20, 2023, shall be distributed pursuant to a plan submitted by the
29 office of temporary and disability assistance and approved by the
30 director of the division of the budget for other eligible services
31 other than the emergency rental assistance program pursuant to part
32 BB of chapter 56 of the laws of 2021, as amended.
33 Funds appropriated herein may be transferred or suballocated to any
34 other state agency or authority.
35 Notwithstanding any inconsistent provision of law, the budget director
36 is hereby authorized to transfer any of the amount appropriated
37 herein to state operations for administration of emergency rental
38 assistance activities (52219) ... 250,000,000 ... (re. $250,000,000)

DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH
AID TO LOCALITIES - REAPPROPRIATIONS 2024-25
1 pursuant to subdivision 5-d of section 2807-k of the public health
2 law shall be distributed in accordance with the provisions below.
3 The commissioner of the department of health shall establish
4 methodologies for determining each facility's relative uncompensated
5 care need amount based on uninsured inpatient and outpatient units
6 of service from the cost reporting year two years prior to the
7 distribution year, multiplied by the applicable medicaid rates in
8 effect January first of the distribution year, as summed and
9 adjusted by a statewide cost adjustment factor and reduced by the
10 sum of all payment amounts collected from such uninsured patients,
11 and as further adjusted by application of a nominal need computation
12 that shall take into account each facility's medicaid inpatient
13 share.
14 Annual distributions pursuant to such regulations for the 2023-2025
15 calendar years shall be in accord with the following: $139,400,000
16 shall be distributed as Medicaid DSH payments to major general
17 public hospitals, and $969,900,000 shall be distributed as Medicaid
18 DSH payments to eligible general hospitals, other than major public
19 general hospitals, for each of the calendar years 2023-2025,
20 provided that the total distributions to eligible general hospitals,
21 other than major public general hospitals, shall be subject to a
22 reduction of $235,400,000 annually, and further provided that
23 eligible general hospitals other than public general hospitals who
24 qualify as enhanced safety net hospitals under section 2807-k of the
25 public health law shall not be subject to such reduction.
26 Such reduction shall be determined by a methodology to be established
27 by the department of health which may take into account the payor
28 mix of each non-public general hospital, including the percentage of
29 inpatient days paid by the medical assistance program.
30 For calendar years 2023-2025, eligible general hospitals other than
31 major public general hospitals that qualify as enhanced safety net
32 hospitals under sections 2087-c and section 2807-k of the public
33 health law which experience a reduction in payments pursuant to
34 section 2807-k of the public health law shall receive a distribution
35 of $64,600,000 annually proportional to the reduction experienced by
36 the facility and pursuant to a methodology determined to the
37 commissioner.
38 Notwithstanding any provision of law to the contrary, the portion of
39 this appropriation covering fiscal year 2023-24 shall supersede and

10 For services and expenses of a quality program for adult care
11 facilities. Such program shall be targeted at facilities with a high

794
DIVISION OF HOUSING AND COMMUNITY RENEWAL
AID TO LOCALITIES - REAPPROPRIATIONS 2024-25
1 created by section 59 of the private housing finance law and admin-
2 istered through community development financial institutions
3 licensed by the New York state department of financial services or
4 domestic not-for-profit corporations that are certified by the
5 United States department of the treasury as community development
6 financial institutions, or for any other purpose authorized by
7 Section 3206 of the American Rescue Plan Act of 2021, Pub.L. 117-2.
8 Such program may provide assistance to homeowners of qualified prop-
9 erties for the purpose of preventing homeowner mortgage deficien-
10 cies, defaults, foreclosures, loss of utilities or home energy
11 services, and displacements of homeowners experiencing financial
12 hardship after January 21, 2020. Such assistance shall include mort-
13 gage payment assistance; financial assistance to allow a homeowner
14 to reinstate a mortgage or pay other housing related costs related
15 to period of forbearance, delinquency, or default; principal
16 reduction; facilitating interest rate reductions; and payment
17 assistance for ancillary homeownership expenses.
18 Of amounts appropriated herein, up to twenty million dollars shall be
19 available each year for three years for a total of up to sixty
20 million dollars for the Homeowner Protection Program to provide
21 homeowners of qualified properties with mortgage relief assistance,
22 assistance with applying for loan modifications or other types of
23 loss mitigation, direct representation in court proceedings and
24 settlement conferences, and homeownership counseling.
25 Notwithstanding any inconsistent provision of the law, the amounts
26 appropriated herein may be suballocated or transferred to any state
27 department, agency, public authority, or community development
28 financial institutions, or domestic not-for-profit corporations that
29 are certified by the United States department of the treasury as
30 community development financial institutions or licensed by the New
31 York state department of financial services for the purposes stated
32 herein. Notwithstanding any provision of state or local law to the
33 contrary, when real property tax payment assistance is provided,
34 with regard to a property with delinquent taxes, the enforcing offi-
35 cer shall accept such assistance as full or partial payment of the
36 delinquent taxes regardless of when the delinquency occurred and
37 shall apply such assistance to any liens against the property

bill_language:
6 By chapter 53, section 1, of the laws of 2023:
7 For prompt assistance to utility customers related to the costs of
8 utility affordability programs authorized by the Public Service
9 Commission and administered by the Department of Public Service, in
10 consultation with the energy affordability policy working group.
11 Additionally, the department shall establish a new, expanded
12 discount program to provide utility bill relief to utility
13 customers, in consultation with the energy affordability policy
14 working group, for residential customers that do not currently
15 qualify for the energy affordability policy program but whose
16 income is below the state median income, provided however that the
17 Public Service Commission shall consider the feasibility of using
18 area median income or other eligibility thresholds in the event the
19 use of state median income prevents reaching all households that
20 have an energy burden greater than six percent. Residential
21 customers of electric corporations regulated by the Public Service
22 Commission and the Long Island Power Authority, and its service
23 provider shall be eligible to participate in the new, expanded
24 discount program. The department shall also establish a program for
25 such purpose to provide an energy affordability guarantee to
26 participating residential customers in the EmPower Plus Program
27 administered by the New York State Energy Research and Development
28 Authority who electrify their homes in accordance with program
29 standards required by the authority; provided, however, that the
30 department is authorized to establish a cap on a residential
31 customer's annual total electric usage by kilowatt hour applicable
32 to the guarantee when establishing such program. The energy
33 affordability guarantee shall provide that any participating
34 residential customers in the EmPower Plus Program shall spend no
35 more than six percent of house-hold income on electric utility
36 bills for the estimated useful life of the related electrification
37 project. The energy affordability guarantee is to remain with the
38 residence that participated in the Empower Plus Program and can
39 transfer between tenants or owners through the electric
40 corporation's application for service, provided however that the
41 bene-fits of this program can only be transferred to eligible new
42 tenants or owners. Amounts appropriated herein may be disbursed to
43 the utilities, including the Long Island Power Authority, on behalf
44 of utility customers as determined by the Department of Public
45 Service based on their share of energy affordability policy program
46 expenditures during calendar year 2022 (48608) ....................
47 200,000,000 .................................... (re. $200,000,000)

20 By chapter 53, section 1, of the laws of 2022:
21 Funds appropriated herein shall be made available for expenses
22 consistent with the purposes of the COVID-19 Pandemic Small Business
23 Seed Funding Grant Program. All or a portion of the funds appropri-
24 ated herein may be suballocated or transferred to any department,
25 agency, or public authority:
26 For services and expenses of the COVID-19 Pandemic Small Business Seed
27 Funding Grant Program. Funds appropriated herein shall be for
28 grants, services, and expenses of a small business seed funding
29 grant program as established under section 16-gg of the New York
30 state urban development corporation act, including costs of program
31 administration, to support viable New York state small businesses,
32 micro-businesses, and for-profit independent arts and cultural
33 organizations that can demonstrate economic hardship as a result of
34 the COVID-19 pandemic.
35 Grants awarded from this appropriation shall be available to eligible
36 entities that do not qualify for business assistance grant programs
37 under the federal American Rescue Plan Act of 2021 or any other
38 available federal COVID-19 economic recovery or business assistance
